spots.name: Mito Kairakuen
spots.note: Japan's premier plum destination — 3,000 trees of 100 varieties in one of Japan's 3 great gardens. Annual Mito Plum Festival draws 300,000 visitors.

spots.name: Atami Plum Garden
spots.note: Japan's earliest plum garden — blooms late January thanks to the warm Izu coast. 60 varieties in a scenic valley. Combine with Atami onsen.

spots.name: Kitano Tenmangu
spots.note: Kyoto's most iconic plum shrine — 1,500 trees dedicated to Sugawara no Michizane. Annual Baika-sai (plum blossom festival) in February. Free to view outside; admission to garden.

spots.name: Yushima Tenjin
spots.note: Central Tokyo plum spot — 300 trees and an annual Bunkyo Ume Matsuri. Popular with students before university entrance exams. Free admission.

spots.name: Hanegi Park
spots.note: 400 plum trees of 60+ varieties in Setagaya. Annual Setagaya Plum Festival in mid-February with food stalls and street performers. Free.

spots.name: Dazaifu Tenmangu
spots.note: Kyushu's most celebrated shrine — 6,000 plum trees including the legendary 'tobi-ume' said to have flown from Kyoto to follow its master. Stunning in February.

spots.name: Osaka Castle Plum Grove
spots.note: 1,270 trees of 100 varieties in the castle park — brilliant white and pink blossoms with the castle tower as backdrop. Free to enter the grove.

spots.name: Jonangu Shrine
spots.note: Famous for breathtaking weeping plum (shidarezume) in the shinden-style garden. One of Kyoto's most photogenic spots in winter. Admission required.

spots.name: Odawara Castle
spots.note: 270 plum trees on the castle grounds with samurai-themed plum festival (Feb-Mar). Great stop between Tokyo and Hakone.

spots.name: Hamarikyu Gardens
spots.note: Former imperial villa with a plum grove near Tokyo Bay — elegant Edo-period garden with views of skyscrapers. Admission required.

spots.name: Tsuruma Park
spots.note: Nagoya's favourite plum park — 200 trees in the historic Meiji-era park. The same park hosts cherry blossoms in April. Free.

spots.name: Ikeda Plum Garden
spots.note: 690 plum trees on an urban hillside in northern Osaka — relaxed local atmosphere, annual plum festival in February. Free admission.

spots.name: Ashikaga Flower Park
spots.note: World-famous 150-year-old great wisteria; features purple, white, and double-petal varieties. Admission required.

spots.name: Kawachi Wisteria Garden
spots.note: Stunning hillside wisteria tunnels in Kitakyushu. Open only during bloom season — check dates before visiting.

spots.name: Kameido Tenjin Shrine
spots.note: Classic Edo-era wisteria festival (Fuji Matsuri) over the drum bridge. Free admission.

spots.name: Byodoin Temple
spots.note: UNESCO World Heritage site famous for its wisteria arbor beside the Phoenix Hall. Very photogenic.

spots.name: Kasuga Taisha Shrine
spots.note: Ancient shrine with a famous wisteria in the museum garden. Near Nara deer park. Free to view from outside.

spots.name: Ushijima Shrine
spots.note: Home to the 1,200-year-old Satte Wisteria — one of Japan's oldest. Free to view.

spots.name: Tatebayashi Tsutsujigaoka Park
spots.note: Over 10,000 azalea and wisteria plants. A large park perfect for walking.

spots.name: Fujinomori Shrine
spots.note: Wisteria tunnel in the shrine grounds. Also famous for iris (June). Free admission.

spots.name: Inokashira Park
spots.note: Wisteria grove near the Benzaiten shrine in the park lake. Great combined with cherry blossom walks. Free.

spots.name: Okazaki Park
spots.note: Adjacent to Okazaki Castle — wisteria arbor at the park. Great with the castle backdrop.

spots.name: Motsuji Temple
spots.note: UNESCO World Heritage temple in Hiraizumi with a beautiful wisteria arbor. Later bloom due to northern location.

spots.name: Nodafuji Garden (Osaka)
spots.note: The original 'Noda-fuji' strain of wisteria originates here; Edo-era artists immortalized this spot.

spots.name: Yamato Koriyama Castle
spots.note: Castle ruins with beautiful wisteria gardens. Combined with the famous goldfish town of Koriyama.

spots.name: Meigetsu-in Temple
spots.note: The 'Hydrangea Temple' of Kamakura — 2,500 blue hydrangea plants lining the main path. Book early.

spots.name: Hasedera Temple (Kamakura)
spots.note: Clifftop temple with sea views and thousands of hydrangea along the slope path. One of Kamakura's best.

spots.name: Mimurotoji Temple
spots.note: 50 varieties and 10,000 hydrangea plants. Called the 'flower temple' of the Uji area.

spots.name: Engakuji Temple
spots.note: Major Rinzai Zen temple in Kita-Kamakura with hydrangea lining the stone steps. Peaceful atmosphere.

spots.name: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu
spots.note: Kamakura's iconic shrine with hydrangea flanking the Dankazura approach path.

spots.name: Sanzenin Temple
spots.note: Mountain temple in Ohara with mossy gardens bursting with hydrangea. Combine with Jakko-in temple.

spots.name: Hakusan Shrine
spots.note: Central Tokyo shrine hosting the annual Bunkyo Hydrangea Festival (Jun 10-16). 3,000 pots of hydrangea.

spots.name: Fujimori Shrine
spots.note: Serene shrine famous for both iris and hydrangea gardens. June is the best time to visit.

spots.name: Mt. Rokko Alpine Botanical Garden
spots.note: Alpine garden at 865m elevation with a large hydrangea collection. Cooler and later than lowland spots.

spots.name: Naritasan Shinshoji Temple
spots.note: Famous temple near Narita Airport with 10,000 hydrangea in the vast park grounds.

spots.name: Hakone Gora Park
spots.note: French formal garden in the mountains above Hakone with hydrangea bloom alongside mountain views.

spots.name: Yatadera Temple
spots.note: Known as the 'Hydrangea Temple of Yamato' — 60 varieties and 10,000 plants on the hillside grounds.

spots.name: Unzen
spots.note: The entire Unzen resort town is filled with wild hydrangea lining the roads and trails. Very scenic.

spots.name: Takahata Fudoson Temple
spots.note: 7,500 hydrangea plants on the hillside behind the temple. Free to enter. Easy from central Tokyo.

spots.name: Hondoji Temple
spots.note: Called the 'Ajisai Temple' of northern Chiba — 50,000 hydrangea spread across a large temple complex.
